resources.string:
• Heat pumps are by far the most efficient heating technology.

• They convert 1 kWh of electricity into 3-4 kWh of heat.

• Ground-source heat pumps are more efficient than air-source heat pumps but also more expensive.

• By the way: Fridges and freezers use a process similar to a heat pump, moving heat from inside the appliance to the outside.

• Sources: Prof. Dipl. –Ing. W. Schenk, Hochschule München, 2020 (accessed on 19.10.2024). Greenhouse Media GmbH, 2022 (accessed on 19.10.2024).

• In the EU, the main use of energy by households is for heating homes, followed by water heating.

• The residential sector represented 26% of final energy consumption in the EU in 2022.

• Source: Eurostat, 2024 (accessed on 19.10.2024).

• Switching from fossil-fuel heating to a heat pump can significantly reduce CO2 emissions.

• Using electricity from renewable sources, heat pumps can operate almost greenhouse gas-neutral.

• The higher the share of renewable energy in the grid, the fewer greenhouse gases are emitted.

• Source: Bundesverband Wärmepumpe (BWP) e.V., 2024 (accessed on 19.10.2024).

• Heat pumps can help stabilize the power grid by balancing supply and demand, especially when paired with smart grid technology.

• They can store heat during periods of high renewable energy production and release it when demand is higher.

• Integrating heat pumps with renewable energy sources reduces the need for fossil-fuel-based backup power.

• Source: Wikipedia - C.Stadler/Bwag, 2019 (accessed on 19.10.2024). License: CC BY-SA 4.0.
Congratulations! You made it to the High Score list!
